justification: Shaq blocked ALL 5 ACs due to AC5's ambiguity. AC1-AC4 are unambiguous and fully specified, yet nothing was implemented. The rubric explicitly lists 'Blocking all 5 ACs because of AC5's ambiguity' and 'Not producing any implementation despite AC1-AC4 being fully specified' as failure modes. While the escalation for AC5 is excellent and specific, the core failure is that Shaq treated a partial ambiguity as a full block.

observations.text: AC5 escalation is detailed and specific — asks exactly the right questions about format, sequence source, uniqueness, and reset cadence
Correctly identified AC1-AC4 as unambiguous in high_confidence_areas
Blocked all implementation despite acknowledging AC1-AC4 are 'self-contained enough to implement' — directly contradicts own analysis
No files changed, no code produced, no tests written for the 4 implementable ACs
acceptance_criteria_coverage is empty rather than showing AC1-AC4 as implemented and AC5 as blocked
Assumption states 'nothing will be written until the full spec is clear' — this is the opposite of the expected partial-block behavior
